channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: In the early going, Trump 2.0 approach on foreign policy is to talk loudly and carry a big stick
item.description: Donald Trump in his return to the White House has already previewed his second term foreign policy approach: Talk loudly and wield a big stick.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Northern Gaza is shattered. The spirit of returning Palestinians is not
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — They walked for hours loaded with whatever clothes, food and blankets they could carry. Many smiled, some hugged loved ones they hadn’t seen for months. Hundreds of thousands of Palestinians filled Gaza’s main coastal road as they streamed back to homes in the north.
item.dc:creator: OSAMA SALEH, ABDEL-KAREEM HANA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EU military chief says it would make sense to put European troops in Greenland, Welt reports
item.description: Top-ranking European military official Robert Brieger reportedly said it would "make perfect sense" to station troops in Greenland amid President Trump's interest in acquiring it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CDC ordered to stop working with WHO immediately, upending expectations of an extended withdrawal
item.description: U.S. public health officials have been told to stop working with the World Health Organization, effective immediately.
item.dc:creator: Mike Stobbe, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Huge health challenges face Gaza residents returning to their homes
item.description: As Gaza residents return to what is left of their homes, they're facing new risks on top of monumental health challenges.
item.dc:creator: Tammy Webber, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Study projects millions of European heat deaths as world warms
item.description: Extreme temperatures — mostly heat — are projected to kill as many as 2.3 million people in Europe by the end of the century unless countries get better at reducing carbon pollution and adapting to hotter conditions, a new study says.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Belarus' President Lukashenko extends rule after election rejected by opposition, EU
item.description: Belarus held an orchestrated election over the weekend that the opposition and the EU rejected as a farce, extending President Lukashenko's more than 30 years in power.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to expect after South Korean president's indictment on rebellion charges
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol has become the country's first leader to be indicted, less than two weeks after he was the first to be detained.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: LIVE: Auschwitz memorial marks 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation
item.description: Nazi German forces murdered some 1.1 million people at the site in southern Poland, which was under German occupation during World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Airliner's final 4 minutes of recordings are missing after crash that killed 179: investigators
item.description: The black boxes of a plane that crashed in South Korea last month failed to record the final four minutes of the flight before the crash that killed 179 people on board, officials said in a preliminary report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Palestinians stream back to northern Gaza after Israel lifts closure
item.description: Thousands of Palestinians are returning to the northern Gaza Strip Monday as part of the cease-fire and hostage release deal between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean plane crash report says bird remains were found in engines, but no cause yet revealed
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The first report on last month’s Jeju Air crash in South Korea confirmed traces of bird strikes in the plane's engines, though officials haven’t determined the cause of the accident that killed all but two of the 181 people on board.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Palestinians celebrate their return to northern Gaza after 15 months of war
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of Palestinians have streamed into heavily destroyed northern Gaza in their first chance to return since the early weeks of the 15-month war with Hamas.
item.dc:creator: By Wafaa Shurafa, Samy Magdy And Joseph Krauss, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Belarus strongman wins a 7th term in an election the opposition calls a farce
item.description: The smiling face of President Alexander Lukashenko gazed out from campaign posters across Belarus on Sunday as the country held an orchestrated election virtually guaranteed to give the 70-year-old autocrat yet another term on top of his three decades in power.
item.dc:creator: YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended: White House
item.description: The White House said that a ceasefire involving Israel and Lebanon had been extended in a Sunday statement. The ceasefire between Hezbollah and Israel, announced by former President Biden in November, is now set “to be in effect until February 18, 2025,” the statement reads.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Holocaust memorial chair: Musk's call for Germany to 'move beyond' Nazi guilt is dangerous
item.description: The chair of Yad Vashem, Israel’s official Holocaust memorial, said Sunday tech billionaire Elon Musk’s call for Germans to “move beyond” the crimes of Nazi Germany is dangerous for the country’s democratic future.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Musk tells Germans to get over 'past guilt' at far-right AfD rally
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk told Germans they should be proud of their heritage and should “move beyond” the “past guilt” of older generations.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Romanian prehistoric gold treasures stolen from Dutch museum
item.description: The Geto-Dacian artifacts belonged to the National History Museum in the capital Bucharest and were on loan to the Drents Museum in the Netherlands.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: A child's life in Gaza, as explained by a Middle East reporter
item.description: Middle East reporter Arwa Damon, who founded the nonprofit INARA, described what she's seen in Gaza to NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Paraguay's disappeared: A dictator's shadow is a roadblock for justice, but a few keep up the fight
item.description: ASUNCIÓN, Paraguay (AP) — Despite being ousted in 1989 after a 35-year reign of terror, during which 20,000 people were tortured, executed or disappeared, some Paraguayans feel as if Gen. Alfredo Stroessner never truly left.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean prosecutors indict impeached president who declared martial law
item.description: Impeached South Korea President Yoon Suk Yeol was indicted on charges of leading an insurrection after briefly declaring martial law last month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants Egypt and Jordan to take in Palestinians from Gaza. Here's why they are likely to refuse
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — President Donald Trump's suggestion that Egypt and Jordan take in Palestinians from the war-ravaged Gaza Strip is likely to be met with a hard “no" from the two U.S. allies and the Palestinians themselves, who fear Israel would never allow them to return.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's world leaders club: who’s in and who's looking to salvage ties
item.description: President Donald Trump's second term is already bringing massive changes as global leaders, allies and adversaries alike, watch to see where they fall in the pecking order and vie for a seat at the table.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea says it tested cruise missile system and vows 'toughest' response to US
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea said Sunday it tested a cruise missile system, its third known weapons display this year, and vowed “the toughest” response to what it called the escalation of U.S.-South Korean military drills that target the North.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN refugee chief calls for more help for Syria as refugees begin to return en masse
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — The United Nations refugee chief said Saturday that some 200,000 refugees have returned to Syria from neighboring countries since the government of Bashar Assad was overthrown last month.
item.dc:creator: OMAR ALBAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas frees 4 female Israeli soldiers in exchange for 200 Palestinian prisoners as ceasefire holds
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Four female Israeli soldiers who were taken in the attack that sparked the war in Gaza returned to Israel on Saturday after Hamas militants paraded them before a crowd of thousands in Gaza City and handed them over to the Red Cross.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, SAM MEDNICK, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Argentina's Milei rails against wokeism, transgenderism in explosive speech at Davos
item.description: Argentinian President Javier Milei called out the radical left for imposing gender ideology on children, during a controversial speech at the World Economic Forum in Switzerland.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tabloids apologize to Prince Harry, admit intruding on Princess Diana
item.description: Prince Harry claimed a monumental victory Wednesday as Rupert Murdoch’s U.K. tabloids made an unprecedented apology.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: At least 76 people killed in a hotel fire at a ski resort in Turkey
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — A fire raged through a 12-story hotel at a popular ski resort in northwestern Turkey early Tuesday during a school holiday, killing at least 76 people — at least two of them when they jumped from the building to escape the flames, officials said.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russia warns Trump against seizing Panama Canal
item.description: Russian officials reaffirmed their commitment to the neutrality of the critical trade route and said it expects both America and Panama to continue respecting that agreement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation
